Gossamer
Features Comparison Quick Start GitHub

Gossamer

A linearly-typed webview shell with provable resource safety

No GC. No refcounting. No runtime overhead.
Just linear types, regions, and compile-time proofs.

View on GitHub Quick Start 
fn main(): I64 =
  let! handle = __ffi("gossamer_create", "Hello", 800, 600, 1, 1, 0) in
  let! _ = __ffi("gossamer_load_html", handle, "<h1>Hello!</h1>") in
  __ffi("gossamer_run", handle)
// handle is CONSUMED by gossamer_run
// using it after this line = compile error

Three guarantees no other framework provides

Linear Resource Ownership

Webview handles, IPC channels, and capability tokens are linear types. Use-after-free and double-free are compile errors, not runtime panics. No Arc, no Rc, no reference counting.

Typed IPC Protocol

The frontend-backend communication protocol is a compile-time type. Message shape mismatches between JavaScript and the backend are impossible. No JSON parsing failures at runtime.

Linear Capability Tokens

Permissions are linear values in the type system. A plugin can't access the filesystem without holding a capability token. The compiler enforces this, not a config file.

How Gossamer compares

Electron Tauri Wails Gossamer
Resource lifecycle GC Arc (refcount) GC Proved (linear)
IPC type safety None Codegen Reflection Compile-time
Permissions Opt-in Runtime JSON None Linear tokens
GC required? Yes No* Yes Never
Binary size 150-300MB 3-8MB 5-10MB 1-3MB

* Tauri uses Arc<Mutex<...>>, which is reference counting.

The stack

Ephapax (.eph) — Application code with linear types
↓ __ffi()
Zig (.zig) — Platform webview bindings (GTK/WebKit/Win32)
↓ C ABI
Idris2 (.idr) — Formal ABI proofs (compile-time only, erased)

At runtime: just Zig + GTK. No GC runtime, no VM, no interpreter overhead in production.

Quick start

# Prerequisites (Fedora)
sudo dnf install gtk3-devel webkit2gtk4.1-devel zig

# Clone and build
git clone https://github.com/hyperpolymath/gossamer
cd gossamer/src/interface/ffi
zig build

# Build the Ephapax compiler
git clone https://github.com/hyperpolymath/ephapax
cd ephapax && cargo build -p ephapax-cli

# Run the hello example
cd gossamer
bash examples/hello/run.sh

Academic paper

Gossamer is backed by formal research. The design, type system, and proofs are documented in an arXiv paper:

Gossamer: A Linearly-Typed Webview Shell with Provable Resource Safety

Jonathan D.A. Jewell, 2026

Read the paper (LaTeX source)

Gossamer is built on Ephapax, a dyadic language with affine and linear types.

MPL-2.0 — © 2026 Jonathan D.A. Jewell (hyperpolymath)
